title: University of Glasgow spinout Quantcore raise £2.5m seed
why:
- Quantcore, a University of Glasgow spin-out, raised £2.5m in seed funding to create a sovereign supply chain for quantum hardware in the UK.
- Quantcore is the only company in the UK manufacturing niobium-based components, which can operate at higher temperatures than aluminium, improving scalability and energy efficiency for quantum technologies.
- The company designs, manufactures, and tests superconducting processors, resonators, and sensors that are central to quantum computers and advanced sensing systems.
- Quantcore’s quantum sensors have applications beyond computing, enabling secure communications and more accurate medical imaging than classical technologies.
- The investment supports the UK’s aim to build domestic quantum technology capacity and follows a national pledge to invest £670m in quantum computing.
